commit.message:
Add SAM3 LoRA fine-tuning pipeline with hypothesis-driven experiment framework

- main.py: full Lightning training loop with LoRA, fp16, W&B, checkpoint support
- src/model/sam3/model.py: SAM3 wrapper with optional LoRA via peft (rank=4, q/v_proj)
- src/model/module.py: LightningModule, trainable-only AdamW, prediction visualisation
- research_plan.yaml: H1 (zero-shot C->C), H2 (zero-shot L->L 3-fold), H3 (LoRA C->C)
- scripts/run_all_experiments.py: YAML-driven runner with per-hypothesis config merging and skip-if-exists logic
- scripts/summarize_results.py: scans outputs/H*/*/metrics.csv, writes summary.csv
- scripts/research_report.py: hypothesis->result->status report from YAML + summary.csv

Zero-shot baselines complete: H1 dice=0.602, H2 mean dice=0.576
H3 LoRA training in progress (batch_size=2, fp16, GTX 1080)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: fix instance size — sequence test ran on Micro, not XL

The sequence-based claiming test (run 6918628) ran on a Micro instance
(2-core, 1GB), not XL. This makes the results more significant: Micro
+ sequence (907 rps) outperforms XL + SKIP LOCKED (839 rps).

commit.message:
Fix /myai redirect leak: convert root page from server to client redirect

The root app/page.js used server-side redirect('/myai') which could
fire during Next.js client-side route transitions to dynamic routes
like /[handle]/ask, causing a brief render then redirect to /myai.

Converted to a client component with useEffect that only redirects
when pathname is actually '/'. This prevents the redirect from leaking
into other route resolutions.

commit.message:
fix: make rate limiter resilient to Redis connection failures

Add swallow_errors=True to slowapi Limiter so the app continues
working when Redis is temporarily unavailable (e.g., during Docker
network recreation). Requests pass through without rate limiting
rather than returning 500 errors.

commit.message:
Set 2bitBridge filter to *.png

Bridge stores Game Boy Camera photos as sequential PNGs in
dcim/1xx_brdg/ folders. Recursive copy preserves folder structure.
Dedup by name+size works since filenames are unique and sequential.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: Separate consecutive GitHub alerts into individual panels

When encountering blank lines between blockquotes, check if the next
blockquote starts a new alert ([!NOTE], [!TIP], etc.). If so, end the
current blockquote and start fresh for the new alert.

This ensures each GitHub alert renders as its own separate Confluence
panel instead of merging multiple alerts into one panel.

commit.message:
fix(arb): don't await cancelled task in validation loop — prevents hang

The await task after task.cancel() was hanging because ccxt/aiohttp
coroutines don't properly propagate CancelledError. Fire-and-forget
the cancel instead — the GC will clean up the abandoned task.

commit.message:
feat: ProjectSkill — autonomous build + IONOS staging deploy

New skill: project (trigger intents: project_create/build/deploy/status/list)

Build flow:
- Creates project dir at /root/sentinel-workspace/projects/{slug}/
- Stores project metadata in new `projects` DB table (auto-created)
- Queues build_project Celery task on tasks_workspace queue
- LLM agent loop (up to 20 rounds, claude-sonnet) scaffolds and writes the
  full project: files, requirements/package.json, README, .gitignore, start.sh
- Always creates start.sh exposing the app on port 8080
- Posts step-by-step build log back to originating Slack thread

Deploy flow (triggered by deploy=true at creation or explicit deploy action):
- Requires confirmation — creates a billable IONOS server
- Provisions Ubuntu 22.04 server via IONOSClient.provision_server()
- Polls IONOS NIC API until public IP assigned (up to 15 min)
- Waits for SSH to accept connections
- Installs system packages based on tech_stack (python/node/go/rust/static)
- Installs Node.js 20 via NodeSource for JS stacks
- SCPs project as tarball → extracts → runs via screen session
- Updates projects table with deploy_ip + deploy_url
- Posts IP to Slack thread and DMs owner

Supporting changes:
- celery_app.py: include app.worker.project_tasks module
- dispatcher.py: register ProjectSkill()
- intent.py: add project_* intents + tech stack detection routing hints

Zanzana MT reconciler: add Prometheus metrics and OTel trace spans (#119518)

* Zanzana MT reconciler: add Prometheus metrics and OTel trace spans

Adds three new Prometheus metrics under iam/authz_zanzana_reconciler:
- namespace_reconcile_duration_seconds (HistogramVec, label: status=success|error)
- work_queue_depth (Gauge)
- tuples_written_total (CounterVec, label: operation=add|delete)

Adds OpenTelemetry child spans for each sub-operation inside a
reconciliation cycle: queueAllNamespaces, fetchGlobalRolePerms,
fetchAndTranslateTuples, fetchAndTranslateGVR (with gvr.group/version/resource
attributes), readAllTuplesFromZanzana, and writeTuplesToZanzana.
span.RecordError is called at every error return path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Reconciler metrics/tracing cleanup

- Replace span.RecordError + manual fmt.Errorf with tracing.Errorf/Error,
  which also sets span status to codes.Error
- Capture time.Since(start) once in runWorker instead of evaluating it
  three times (two log calls + metric observation)
- Cache WithLabelValues child counters before batch loops to avoid
  repeated map lookups per batch

  lazy initializer to avoid synchronous setState in an effect

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: Double data-unwrapping in API layer causing crashes across pages

The Axios interceptor already unwraps { success, data } envelopes, but
11 API methods (chat, anomaly, prediction, briefing, approval) did
an additional .data.data access, resulting in undefined values and
component crashes. Standardized all API methods to use toArray() for
lists and safe fallback for single objects.

Also adds missing "use client" directives to button.tsx and label.tsx
(radix-ui imports), standardizes store data access patterns, and adds
a dashboard error boundary for graceful error handling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace back button with hamburger menu on admin and super-admin pages

Remove showBack from all admin and super-admin list pages so the default
hamburger menu navigation is shown instead, consistent with general user
pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve cooldown log spam and bar aggregator callback loss (v1.1.7)

Two critical production issues fixed:

Issue 1: Repeated cooldown logs (60+ duplicates)
- Root cause: cooldown execute() had no return after completion
- Result: orchestrator called execute() in tight loop
- Fix: added explicit return statement to prevent loop

Issue 2: No real-time bars after warmup (no ORB notifications)
- Root cause: error handler cleared bar_aggregators dict
- Result: destroyed callback objects, breaking data flow
- Fix: removed aggregator clearing, added reset in warmup phase

Additional improvements:
- Warmup now resets cooldown manager state (Step 0/6)
- Prevents stale cooldown state from previous trading day
- Added comprehensive validation tests

Tested locally with 4 validation tests + 3 integration tests (all pass)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Migrate heightmap storage from 64x64 (cell-based) to 65x65 (vertex-based)

Adjacent tiles now overlap by one row/column at boundaries, matching the
convention used by Unity and Unreal. This eliminates cross-tile lookups for
edge vertices and fixes water tile seams at tile boundaries.

- server/src/terrain/defaults.rs: VERTS_PER_SIDE=65, HEIGHTMAP_SIZE=8450 bytes
- client terrainHeightManager.ts: 65-stride indexing, edge data sync written
  directly into neighbor buffers before geometry refresh, simplified padding loop
- client terrainGenerator.ts: sliceIntoTiles outputs 65x65 vertex heightmaps
- client GenerateTerrainDialog.svelte: edge data read with 65-stride
- client water-material.ts: UV remapping for 65x65 texture sampling
- client GameSceneWaterLayer.svelte: refresh adjacent water tiles on load/edit
- tools/import_heightmap.py: extract 65x65 vertex chunks, assert 8450-byte output

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add targetCycles to DoorConfigRequest and pass through all config dicts

The root cause of springs mismatching between specs tab and quote was
that targetCycles was not included in DoorConfigRequest or the config
dicts built for get-part-numbers, generate-quote, and get-parts-for-quote
endpoints. This caused spring calculation to default to 10,000 cycles
instead of the user-selected value (e.g. 25,000), selecting a smaller
spring. Also added targetCycles to customer_portal config dicts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: rename tools from law_* prefix to standard names

The German Law MCP was the only law MCP using non-standard law_* prefixed
tool names (e.g. law_search_documents instead of search_legislation). The
portal expects standard names and silently fell back to a 6-law hardcoded
DB when it received JSON-RPC "method not found" errors.

Changes:
- Rename 19 tools to match the standard law MCP naming convention
- Remove 3 multi-country tools (list_countries, describe_country, run_ingestion)
- Remove country parameter from all tools (hardcode "de")
- Update tool descriptions cross-references (e.g. "Use get_provision to...")
- Update golden test fixtures, unit tests, and README

commit.message:
refine: handle NotFound from revoke/rename device key mutations as 404 (#417)

Both revoke_device and rename_device verify ownership via get_owned_device
before calling the mutation. If the device is deleted between those two
calls (TOCTOU race), the repo returns DeviceKeyRepoError::NotFound. The
previous catch-all logged an error and returned 500, which is incorrect—
a missing record is not a server error. Handle NotFound explicitly with
404 in both handlers and add regression tests for this path.

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Income-based FI model: perpetuity formula + W-2 stops at FI

- Replace annuity PV with perpetuity (netAnnualNeed / returnRate) for
  required capital: FI now means portfolio income covers expenses
  indefinitely without drawing down principal
- Zero out salary and bonus in the quarter after FI is first detected,
  reflecting the user's choice to discontinue full-time employment
- Guard FI detection on annualSpending > 0 to require a defined
  spending goal before FI can be achieved
- 253 tests passing

commit.message:
feat: add Sainte-Laguë seat allocation for voting districts

Aggregate LGA vote counts to 150 voting districts (summing constituent
LGA votes) and allocate 622 seats using the Sainte-Laguë (Webster)
method.  District seat counts come from seat_allocation.xlsx and LGA-to-
district mappings from voting_districts_summary.xlsx.

New functions in results.py:
- sainte_lague(): pure divisor-method allocation (1, 3, 5, 7, ...)
- allocate_district_seats(): LGA→district aggregation + seat allocation

run_election.py now prints per-district seats, national seat totals with
vote/seat proportionality, and a zonal seat breakdown.
